title: Find the Next Greater Element in Array

statement.markdown:
You are given an array of integers. For each element in the array, find the next greater element for it. The next greater element of a number `x` is the smallest number that is greater than `x` and also in the same array. If there is no such greater number, assign `0` to it.

Example:

Input: `[4, 5, 2, 10]`
Output: `[5, 10, 10, 0]`

Implement the solution in the provided code.
statement.outputFormat: The array with each element replaced by the next greater element for it
statement.notes:
Time complexity O(n) where n is the size of the input array. Space complexity O(1) as no additional space is used.

Note: The array may contain duplicate elements.

examples.explanation: The next greater element of 4 is 5, of 5 is 10, of 2 is 10 and of 10 is 0
The next greater element of 1 is 2, of 2 is 3, of 3 is 4 and of 4 is 0
The next greater element of 1 is 3, of 3 is 2, of 1 is 2 and of 2 is 0

hints.text: Use a stack to keep track of the elements.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to use a stack to keep track of the elements. We start from the right of the array and push the elements onto the stack if they are smaller than the current element. If the current element is greater than the top of the stack, we pop the stack and update the next greater element for the top of the stack with the current element.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to compare each element with every other element in the array and update the next greater element for each element.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a stack to keep track of the elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proved by showing that the stack always contains the elements that have not found their next greater element yet.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run of the solution would be to apply the solution to the example inputs and show the output.
commonMistakes: Not updating the next greater element correctly

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> nextGreaterElement(vector<int>& nums); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int[] nextGreaterElement(int[] nums); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def nextGreaterElement(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[int]: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> nextGreaterElement(vector<int>& nums) { vector<int> res(nums.size()); for (int i = nums.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--) { int index = 0; while (index < i && nums[i] > nums[index]) index++; res[i] = nums[index]; } return res; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int[] nextGreaterElement(int[] nums) { int[] res = new int[nums.length]; for (int i = nums.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) { int index = 0; while (index < i && nums[i] > nums[index]) index++; res[i] = nums[index]; } return res; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def nextGreaterElement(self, nums): res = [0] * len(nums) for i in range(len(nums) - 1, -1, -1): index = 0 while index < i and nums[i] > nums[index]: index += 1 res[i] = nums[index] return res
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function nextGreaterElement(nums) { let res = new Array(nums.length).fill(0); for (let i = nums.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) { let index = 0; while (index < i && nums[i] > nums[index]) index++; res[i] = nums[index]; } return res; }

solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> nextGreaterElement(vector<int>& nums) { vector<int> res(nums.size()); for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { int nextGreater = 0; for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.size(); j++) if (nums[j] > nums[i]) { nextGreater = nums[j]; break; } res[i] = nextGreater; } return res; } };
solutions.java: public class Solution { public int[] nextGreaterElement(int[] nums) { int[] res = new int[nums.length]; for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { int nextGreater = 0; for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.length; j++) if (nums[j] > nums[i]) { nextGreater = nums[j]; break; } res[i] = nextGreater; } return res; } }
solutions.python: class Solution: def nextGreaterElement(self, nums): res = [0] * len(nums) for i in range(len(nums)): nextGreater = 0 for j in range(i + 1, len(nums)): if nums[j] > nums[i]: nextGreater = nums[j] break res[i] = nextGreater return res
solutions.javascript: function nextGreaterElement(nums) { let res = new Array(nums.length).fill(0); for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { let nextGreater = 0; for (let j = i + 1; j < nums.length; j++) if (nums[j] > nums[i]) { nextGreater = nums[j]; break; } res[i] = nextGreater; } return res; }
